text.title: Rails Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real Rails 7+ Service Object codebases. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Controller switches on `result.ok?` for some services and `result.success?` for others; a refactor breaks one path silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Each service defined its own `ServiceResult`-like class with subtly different API.
antipattern.fix: Lock the canonical class. Add a CI grep failing on additional `class ServiceResult` or alternative APIs (`def ok?`).

antipattern.symptom: Welcome email sent; user record rolled back; user complains the link is broken.
antipattern.fix: Move dispatch to `after_create_commit` callback OR chain a follow-on Sidekiq job after the service's transaction returns success.

antipattern.symptom: New endpoint shipped without `authorize`; an unauthorised user updates other tenants' records.
antipattern.fix: Enable `verify_authorized` + `verify_policy_scoped` in `ApplicationController`. Add a request spec that asserts every action triggers Pundit.

antipattern.symptom: `User#after_save` runs business logic (charge card + send email); test isolation breaks; the callback fires from seed scripts unexpectedly.
antipattern.root-cause: Business logic placed in a callback for "convenience".
antipattern.fix: Restrict callbacks to invariants (`before_validation :normalize_email`). Move business logic into the Service Object.

antipattern.symptom: N+1 ships; Brakeman warns about SQL injection that nobody saw; an outdated gem with a known CVE stays on `Gemfile.lock` for months.
antipattern.root-cause: CI did not run Bullet / Brakeman / bundler-audit.
antipattern.fix: Add `bundle exec brakeman --no-pager --exit-on-warn` + `bundle exec bundler-audit check --update` + Bullet enabled in test env. Gate merges on green.
